commands.repair_reason: Contributor action is proof-only: add redacted real Control UI approval proof for the latest UI change; no automated repair is appropriate.; Cleared: The diff narrows a Codex execution path and does not add dependencies, workflow permission changes, package resolution changes, lifecycle hooks, or secret handling. commands.review_summary:
The PR gates Codex CLI session resume behind plugin approval, propagates restricted approval decisions through gateway and Control UI approval handling, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. as a source-level reproduction: current main's Codex resume node-invoke policy calls `ctx.invokeNode()` directly, and the node handler can run `codex exec resume` after validating the session id and prompt. I did not run a live current-main resume in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is proof-only: add redacted real Control UI approval proof for the latest UI change; no automated repair is appropriate.

target.title: fix(codex): gate CLI session resume behind plugin approval
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex CLI session resume did not consistently pass through the same approval gate used for high-impact Codex actions.
- Why it matters: resuming a local Codex CLI session may run a new prompt in an existing local session, so the policy should require explicit operator approval before node dispatch.
- What changed: the resume policy now requests plugin approval limited to `allow-once` and `deny` before calling `ctx.invokeNode()`, and does not dispatch when approval delivery is unavailable or denied.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): session listing remains unchanged; the node-host resume implementation and Codex CLI spawn behavior are unchanged.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: N/A
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex CLI session resume now requires plugin approval before node dispatch.
- Real environment tested: local macOS development checkout, branch `codex-resume-approval`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `npm exec --yes pnpm@11.1.0 -- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/node-cli-sessions.test.ts`
  - `npm exec --yes pnpm@11.1.0 -- oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/node-cli-sessions.ts extensions/codex/src/node-cli-sessions.test.ts`
  - Started a real local Gateway with the bundled `codex` plugin loaded.
  - Started a real headless node host process against that Gateway.
  - Approved the node command surface containing `codex.cli.session.resume` and `codex.cli.sessions.list`.
  - Invoked both Codex CLI node commands through the Gateway `node.invoke` path.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
[gateway] http server listening (10 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, codex, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice)
[proof ...] approval runtime client connected
[proof ...] operator invoke client connected
[proof ...] starting headless node host codex-proof-node-...
[gateway] device pairing auto-approved device=<node-device-id> role=node
[proof ...] approved node pairing request ... commands=["system.run.prepare","system.run","system.which","browser.proxy","codex.cli.session.resume","codex.cli.sessions.list"]
[proof ...] node connected: nodeId=<node-device-id> commands include codex.cli.session.resume after node-pair approval
[proof ...] non-dangerous list invoke succeeded: sessionCount=1 payloadJSON=present
[proof ...] plugin approval requested: id=plugin:... title="Resume Codex CLI session" severity="critical" toolName="codex.cli.session.resume"
[ws] ⇄ res ✗ node.invoke ... errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=Codex CLI session resume requires plugin approval.
[proof ...] plugin approval resolved with deny: id=plugin:...
[proof ...] deny resume result error: {"name":"GatewayClientRequestError","gatewayCode":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"Codex CLI session resume requires plugin approval.","details":{"approvalId":"plugin:...","decision":"deny","code":"PLUGIN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED"}}
[proof ...] plugin approval requested: id=plugin:... title="Resume Codex CLI session" severity="critical" toolName="codex.cli.session.resume"
[proof ...] plugin approval resolved with allow-once: id=plugin:...
[ws] ⇄ res ✗ node.invoke ... errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=Error: Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id.
[proof ...] allow-once resume result error: {"name":"GatewayClientRequestError","gatewayCode":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"Error: Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id.","details":{"nodeError":{"code":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"Error: Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id."},"code":"INVALID_REQUEST"}}
[proof ...] proof completed
```

- Observed result after fix:
  - `codex.cli.sessions.list` dispatched successfully through the paired node without approval.
  - `codex.cli.session.resume` created a plugin approval request before node dispatch.
  - A denied approval returned `PLUGIN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED` and did not dispatch to the node.
  - An `allow-once` approval proceeded to the node handler.
  - The proof used an intentionally invalid session id, so the node handler returned `Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id` before any Codex CLI resume or model turn could run.
- What was not tested: full Web UI approval click-through. Approval decisions were resolved via the Gateway plugin approval RPC from an approval-runtime client.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): On the previous implementation, the Codex resume node-invoke policy dispatched without creating a plugin approval request; this patch adds the approval request before dispatch.

Exact steps run after this patch:

```text
npm exec --yes pnpm@11.1.0 -- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/node-cli-sessions.test.ts
npm exec --yes pnpm@11.1.0 -- oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/node-cli-sessions.ts extensions/codex/src/node-cli-sessions.test.ts
```

Observed result: denied approval and unavailable approval runtime do not call `invokeNode`; `allow-once` forwards to `invokeNode`. Targeted regression tests and formatting check completed successfully locally.

### Live Gateway run

Run locally on macOS from branch `codex-resume-approval` with a real local Gateway, bundled `codex` plugin, and a real headless node host process. The run used a temporary local OpenClaw state/config, approved the node command surface, and invoked the Codex CLI node commands through the Gateway `node.invoke` path.

Command run:

```text
node --import tsx .artifacts/codex-resume-real-behavior-proof.ts
```

Redacted terminal output:

```text
[gateway] http server listening (10 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, codex, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice)
[proof ...] approval runtime client connected
[proof ...] operator invoke client connected
[proof ...] starting headless node host codex-proof-node-...
[gateway] device pairing auto-approved device=<node-device-id> role=node
[proof ...] approved node pairing request ... commands=["system.run.prepare","system.run","system.which","browser.proxy","codex.cli.session.resume","codex.cli.sessions.list"]
[proof ...] node connected: nodeId=<node-device-id> commands include codex.cli.session.resume after node-pair approval
[proof ...] non-dangerous list invoke succeeded: sessionCount=1 payloadJSON=present
[proof ...] plugin approval requested: id=plugin:... title="Resume Codex CLI session" severity="critical" toolName="codex.cli.session.resume"
[ws] ⇄ res ✗ node.invoke ... errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=Codex CLI session resume requires plugin approval.
[proof ...] plugin approval resolved with deny: id=plugin:...
[proof ...] deny resume result error: {"name":"GatewayClientRequestError","gatewayCode":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"Codex CLI session resume requires plugin approval.","details":{"approvalId":"plugin:...","decision":"deny","code":"PLUGIN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED"}}
[proof ...] plugin approval requested: id=plugin:... title="Resume Codex CLI session" severity="critical" toolName="codex.cli.session.resume"
[proof ...] plugin approval resolved with allow-once: id=plugin:...
[ws] ⇄ res ✗ node.invoke ... errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=Error: Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id.
[proof ...] allow-once resume result error: {"name":"GatewayClientRequestError","gatewayCode":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"Error: Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id.","details":{"nodeError":{"code":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"Error: Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id."},"code":"INVALID_REQUEST"}}
[proof ...] proof completed
```

Observed result:

- `codex.cli.sessions.list` dispatched successfully through the paired node without approval.
- `codex.cli.session.resume` created a plugin approval request before node dispatch.
- A denied approval returned `PLUGIN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED` and did not dispatch to the node.
- An `allow-once` approval proceeded to the node handler.
- The proof used an intentionally invalid session id, so the node handler returned `Missing or invalid Codex CLI session id` before any Codex CLI resume or model turn could run.

What was not tested:

- Full Web UI approval click-through. Approval decisions were resolved via the Gateway plugin approval RPC from an approval-runtime client.
- Full live gateway + paired node + Web UI approval round trip.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: the Codex CLI resume node-invoke policy did not request plugin approval before dispatching to the node.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression test covering approval behavior for the Codex CLI resume policy.
- Contributing context: session listing and session resume are registered together, but only resume needs approval gating.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/node-cli-sessions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test locks in: resume requires plugin approval, does not dispatch when approval delivery is unavailable, and forwards only after an allow decision.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the changed behavior is local to the Codex node-invoke policy handler.
- Existing test that already covers this: none before this PR.

## User-Visible / Behavior Changes

Codex CLI session resume through this node command now requires plugin approval before dispatch.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
resume request -> node dispatch

After:
resume request -> plugin approval -> node dispatch
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes — this narrows the resume execution path by requiring plugin approval before node dispatch.
- Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Mostly
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- Notes: existing automation that invokes `codex.cli.session.resume` may now need an approval-capable runtime or an explicit approval decision.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: existing automation that resumes Codex CLI sessions may now stop for approval.
  - Mitigation: the change is limited to session resume; session listing and node-host resume behavior remain unchanged after approval.

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: deny blocks dispatch; unavailable approval runtime blocks dispatch; allow-once forwards dispatch.
- Edge cases checked: approval runtime unavailable.
- What was not verified: full Web UI approval click-through.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
target.author: laphilosophie
